name: India
summary: Day 75 of Hormuz blockade. **INDIAN MARKETS EXTENDED LOSSES DAY 2 (May 12 close): SENSEX 74,559.24 (-1,456 PTS / -1.92%) = LARGEST 2-SESSION DRAWDOWN OF CYCLE; cumulative ~16 LAKH CRORE m-cap erosion across 4 sessions (BusinessToday); Nifty 50 closed 23,379.55 (-436.3 pts / -1.83%), broke below 23,500 support that had held since May 5-6 stabilisation; Bank Nifty -1.0%. USD/INR fresh closing record ₹95.63 (vs ₹95.54 May 12 intraday in yesterday's brief); intraday hovering ₹95.58; ₹95.50 RBI direct-intervention threshold now BROKEN IN CLOSE PRINTS, not just intraday. FIIs net sellers (continuation, not reversal). **MOODY'S CUT INDIA FY26 GROWTH 0.8PP TO 6.0% (from 6.8%)** citing subdued private consumption, capital formation, industrial activity amid higher energy costs — SOVEREIGN-RATING ACTOR moving direction (materially harder than yesterday's 'global brokerage' 6.7%→6.2% flag).** May 13 intraday OPENED with relief bid (Sensex +700 pts from day's low, Nifty above 23,450 per BS LIVE morning, markets dossier shows close prints reflecting recovery: Nifty 23,496.80 +0.5%, Sensex 74,813.40 +0.3%, Bank Nifty 53,974.00 +0.8% — but this is intraday recovery from the May 12 collapse, NOT a regime reversal; cumulative drawdown still -1.83% from May 11 peak. **A11 IRAN FM ARAGHCHI ARRIVES NEW DELHI MAY 13 FOR BRICS FM MEETING (May 14-15); MEETS MODI MAY 14 — SAME DAY TRUMP IN BEIJING, DAY BEFORE MODI FLIES TO UAE.** India holds 2026 BRICS presidency; agenda 'regional stability, multilateral cooperation, economic resilience' = code for India offering Iran non-aligned channel that is NOT Trump-led process. The most overt Indian strategic-autonomy choreography of cycle: receive Iran's FM while travelling to Iran's covert-combatant Gulf rival 3 days later, while Trump simultaneously courts/confronts Xi over the same crisis. **B5 PM MODI 5-NATION TOUR MAY 15-20 CONFIRMED — UAE first leg, then Netherlands, Sweden, Norway, Italy; UAE leg explicit focus on 'energy security for India amid disruptions in global supply chains due to US-Iran war' (Khaleej Times) + Comprehensive Strategic Partnership; European leg = energy + emerging tech + defence. UAE leg complicated by A10 — Newsweek+WSJ: Saudi Arabia ALSO conducted secret strikes on Iran late March (REPORTED); Modi will land in UAE knowing UAE is now a confirmed covert combatant AND Saudi reportedly so as well.** **UNION MINISTER HARDEEP PURI QUANTIFIED OMC PAIN OFFICIALLY FOR FIRST TIME: PSU OIL COMPANIES LOSING ₹1,000 CRORE PER DAY; TOTAL UNDER-RECOVERY ON TRACK FOR ₹1.98 LAKH CRORE** (Business Standard primary). India has REINTRODUCED COVID-ERA WORK-FROM-HOME RULES as fuel-conservation measure (Fortune primary). **Republic/Times Now/Zee continued domestic politics framing on WFH reinstatement as 'Modi's swadeshi push' rather than crisis-cause framing — propaganda outlets protecting government narrative on cause-of-pain question. NDTV (post-Adani) ran Modi 5-nation tour with energy-security framing and ZERO engagement of UAE's covert combatant status — consistent with post-acquisition editorial restraint. No Indian outlet has editorialised against PM Modi visiting UAE knowing UAE struck Iran — Modi's diaspora base in UAE (~3.5M Indians) makes anti-UAE framing politically unworkable. Indian government framing through ANI: India's BRICS presidency + Araghchi visit = 'strategic autonomy' working — receiving Iran AND visiting UAE in the same week as confirmation that India is the working multi-vector channel.** India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-7.00% est. flat (Moody's cut absorbed by bond market without further spike). RBI reserves drawdown next print due ~May 16. Sectoral: PSU OMCs continuing under-recovery pressure; Reliance refining-margin tailwind; Nifty IT -3% intraday May 12 (US AI-pricing-pressure overhang); Nifty Pharma -3% YTD vs Nifty -12% YTD (defensive bid intact). **STRUCTURAL STORY: US (S&P 500 -0.29% off record on CPI absorption) + Korea (KOSPI +2.8% bounce after May 12 -1.7%) running their own cycles while India takes the full brunt of the energy-cascade — worst import-FII-currency triple-vulnerability of major economies. The Moody's cut adds a sovereign-rating channel to the structural FII-flow case that wasn't there a week ago.** Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: requires (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) INR retracement to ₹93. None imminent. ORIGINAL SUMMARY CONTEXT (May 12): Sensex closed May 11 at 76,015.28 (-1,312.91 pts, -1.70%); — Sensex closed May 11 at 76,015.28 (-1,312.91 pts, -1.70%) = LARGEST SINGLE-DAY POINT DROP OF CYCLE; YTD drop crossed 10,000 points; Nifty 23,815.85 (-360.30 pts, -1.49%); Bank Nifty 54,439.90 (-870.65) before V-shape May 12 recovery to 56,565.70 (+0.85%); USD/INR ₹95.0930 May 11 → ₹95.54 May 12 (intraday low ₹95.32 record) — ₹95 STRUCTURAL TRIGGER RE-BREACHED, CLEANLY BROKE RBI'S ₹95.50 DIRECT-INTERVENTION THRESHOLD; FII single-day net sell ₹8,437.56 cr May 11 (highest since April 24); YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 LAKH CR (already exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr).** Market dossier C2 assessment: India deployment timing being pushed OUT, not forward, by today's tape. The cascade is now self-reinforcing: weaker rupee → imported inflation → bond yields up → debt-FPI also out → more INR pressure. **B8 PM MODI HYDERABAD ADDRESS MAY 10-11: seven-appeal package — (i) reduce fuel consumption (work from home), (ii) defer foreign travel for one year, (iii) defer gold purchases (incl. weddings) for one year, (iv) reduce edible oil consumption, (v) cut fertilizer ~50% (mixed organic), (vi) push swadeshi, (vii) general consumption restraint** — first major Indian-government public engagement of cycle, targeting two specific FX pressure points (gold imports ~$50bn/yr drain + crude import bill). Al Jazeera framed 'Iran war effect: Why is Modi asking Indians to avoid foreign trips, gold?' Indian media (Business Standard, Livemint, Indian Express) received as sober/serious, NOT panic. MEA separately issued 'deeply concerned' statement urging restraint and calling for 'unimpeded' Hormuz trade. **B16 PM Modi 5-nation tour May 15-20: UAE, Netherlands, Sweden, Norway, Italy** — explicit energy/defence/trade diversification; UAE leg materially complicated by WSJ Lavan revelation (B6). **B13 INDIA FY27 GDP DOWNGRADE TO 6.2% FROM 6.7%** (global brokerage May 11; 'historically large energy shock with asymmetric macro risks'). **RBI forex reserves -$7.79bn to $690.69bn week ended May 1 (sharp drawdown, active intervention); reserves down ~$38bn from late-Feb peak of $728.49bn.** India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-6.99%; new 10Y auction cut-off near 6.95% (auction-anchored despite crude bid). Sectoral: PSU OMCs (BPCL -2.34%, HPCL -2.65%, IOC -2.45% May 11) face Q1 FY27 losses large enough to wipe full-year FY26 profits per brokerage flags; Reliance May 12 intraday +1.99% (refining margin tailwind diverges from OMC marketing-margin compression); Nifty IT -3% May 12 intraday (rupee weakness usually a tailwind but US-growth fears dominating); Nifty Pharma -3% YTD vs Nifty -12% YTD (defensive bid intact). **STRUCTURAL STORY: US (S&P 500 fresh record, NASDAQ +14.7% 1M on AI) and Korea (KOSPI +33% 1M on AI semis) running their own cycles while India takes the whole brunt of the energy-cascade — worst import-FII-currency triple-vulnerability of major economies.** Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: requires (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) INR retracement to ₹93 handle. None imminent. Goldman 2026 Brent average $85 implicitly requires Q4 average $71 — bullish case is 'back half of year.' Bearish case: no deal + kinetic escalation = INR ₹96-97 + Nifty toward 22,000.

current.deployment_context: WORST POSITIONING OF MAJOR ECONOMIES. India takes whole brunt of energy-cascade pressure — worst import-FII-currency triple-vulnerability. PSU OMC Q1 FY27 losses could wipe full-year FY26 profits per brokerage flags. RBI reserves -$7.79bn week ended May 1 = active intervention not stopping. Modi seven-appeal address signals FX seriousness. FY27 GDP cut 6.7%→6.2%. US-vs-India decoupling is STRUCTURAL story of the day. Deployment requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + FII positive + INR ₹93 — none imminent. Bearish scenario: ₹96-97 INR + Nifty 22,000 if no deal + kinetic escalation.

active_triggers.condition: Rupee crosses ₹95/USD — DEACTIVATED on ceasefire. Current ₹92.55. WATCHING.
India announces fuel-price pass-through OR Iran-rupee oil track restart via Chabahar within 30 days (by 2026-05-27)

recent_signals.headline: DAY 2 CASCADE EXTENSION: Sensex closed May 12 at 74,559.24 (-1,456 pts, -1.92%) = LARGEST 2-SESSION DRAWDOWN OF CYCLE; cumulative ~16 LAKH CRORE m-cap erosion across 4 sessions; Nifty 50 closed 23,379.55 (-436.3 pts, -1.83%), BROKE BELOW 23,500 SUPPORT that had held since May 5-6 stabilisation; decline broad — IT, realty, consumer durables led down (IT sold on AI-pricing-pressure overhang). **USD/INR FRESH CLOSING RECORD ₹95.63 (vs ₹95.54 May 12 intraday in yesterday's brief); ₹95.50 RBI direct-intervention threshold NOW BROKEN IN CLOSE PRINTS, NOT JUST INTRADAY.** FIIs continued net sellers; multiple outlets confirm continuation rather than reversal. **MOODY'S CUT INDIA FY26 GROWTH 0.8PP TO 6.0% (from 6.8%)** citing subdued private consumption, capital formation, industrial activity amid higher energy costs — SOVEREIGN-RATING ACTOR (materially harder than yesterday's brokerage 6.7%→6.2% flag). May 13 intraday opened with relief bid (Sensex +700 pts from low, Nifty above 23,450 per BS LIVE; close prints in markets dossier: Nifty 23,496.80 +0.5%, Sensex 74,813.40 +0.3%, Bank Nifty 53,974.00 +0.8%) BUT this is intraday recovery from May 12 collapse, NOT regime reversal — cumulative drawdown still -1.83% from May 11 peak. **Union Minister HARDEEP PURI QUANTIFIED OMC PAIN FOR FIRST TIME: PSU oil companies losing ₹1,000 CRORE PER DAY; total under-recovery on track for ₹1.98 LAKH CRORE.** India has REINTRODUCED COVID-ERA WORK-FROM-HOME RULES as fuel-conservation measure (Fortune primary; Republic/Times Now/Zee continued domestic-politics framing as 'Modi's swadeshi push' rather than crisis-cause framing). **IRAN FM ARAGHCHI ARRIVES NEW DELHI MAY 13 FOR BRICS FM MEETING (May 14-15); MEETS MODI MAY 14 — same day Trump in Beijing, day before Modi flies to UAE.** India holds 2026 BRICS presidency. Agenda 'regional stability, multilateral cooperation, economic resilience' = code for India offering Iran non-aligned channel that is NOT Trump-led process. Most overt Indian strategic-autonomy choreography of cycle. **B5 PM MODI 5-NATION TOUR MAY 15-20 CONFIRMED — UAE (Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, explicit 'energy security amid US-Iran war' framing + Comprehensive Strategic Partnership), Netherlands, Sweden, Norway, Italy (energy + emerging tech + defence).** UAE leg materially complicated by A10: Newsweek + WSJ now also report Saudi Arabia conducted secret strikes on Iran late March (REPORTED) — Modi will land in UAE (confirmed covert combatant) AND visit Iran's covert-combatant Gulf rivals in same week the Iranian FM is at Delhi BRICS table. No Indian outlet has editorialised against Modi visiting UAE knowing UAE struck Iran — diaspora-base in UAE (~3.5M Indians) makes anti-UAE framing politically unworkable. **India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-7.00% est. flat** (Moody's cut absorbed without further bond spike). Reversibility asymmetric: requires Brent <$95 + FII positive + INR ₹93 — none imminent. Sonal Varma (Nomura) call validated TWICE — ₹95.54 May 12 + ₹95.63 May 12 close; ₹96 trigger sits within 5-7 sessions per graph.

recent_signals.headline: SUNDAY HOLIDAY — INDIAN MARKETS CLOSED; FRIDAY MAY 8 CLOSE STILL OPERATIVE: Nifty 24,176.15 (-0.6%); Sensex 77,328.19 (-0.7%); Bank Nifty 55,310.55 (-1.3% LEADING). FII outflows ₹340.89 cr May 8; ₹1.92 LAKH CR YTD CY26 already exceeds full-year 2025 (₹1.66 lakh cr). FPI ownership ~16%, lowest in 2 decades. INR ₹94.47 stable; India 10Y G-Sec ~7.0%. **No fresh Indian-source diplomatic engagement piece today. The intelligence relevant for India today is silence: no MEA statement, no PM Modi statement, no Jaishankar statement, no public India position on Qatar mediator emergence, the bulk carrier strike near Doha, the Bahrain detentions, or the Pezeshkian meeting claim. India's 'strategic autonomy' posture continues; the absence is the position.** Eight-million-strong Indian Gulf diaspora exposure factor remains active.
sources: Searched MEA / PMO / EAM Twitter handles — CONFIRMED ABSENCE of statement

recent_signals.headline: INDIAN MARKETS — Nifty 24,360 (+0.12%) / Sensex 77,949 (-0.01%); Gift Nifty +0.67% pre-open.
